title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s. A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards.
constraints: s consist of only digits and English letters
thinking_questions: What's the center of a palindrome? Can it be a character or between characters?
How do you expand outward from a center to find the longest palindrome?
Why is expand-around-center O(n^2) but O(1) space, while DP is O(n^2) time and space?

hints.L1: Every palindrome has a center — either a single character (odd length) or between two characters (even length).
hints.L2: Expand-around-center: for each of 2n-1 possible centers, expand left/right while characters match. Track the longest found. O(n^2) time, O(1) space.
hints.L3:
# Longest Palindromic Substring — O(n^2) time, O(1) space
# KEY INSIGHT: try every possible center (2n-1 centers for n chars)

def longestPalindrome(s):
    res = ""

    def expand(l, r):
        # Expand while in bounds and chars match
        while l >= 0 and r < len(s) and s[l] == s[r]:
            l -= 1
            r += 1
        return s[l+1:r]  # slice after last valid expansion

    for i in range(len(s)):
        odd = expand(i, i)      # odd-length: center at char i
        even = expand(i, i+1)   # even-length: center between i and i+1

        if len(odd) > len(res): res = odd
        if len(even) > len(res): res = even

    return res
# WHY 2n-1 centers: n odd centers + n-1 even centers

follow_up_questions: How does Manacher's algorithm solve this in O(n)?
What's the DP approach and why is it O(n^2) space?
How does this differ from Longest Palindromic Subsequence?
solution_notes: Expand-around-center. 2n-1 centers, expand while matching. O(n^2) time, O(1) space. Better than DP which needs O(n^2) space.
